hypothesis: If a chemically-informed autoencoder pretrained on materials data is used as the latent space for a conditional diffusion model (CompGen) conditioned on adsorbate identity and adsorption energy, then the model will generate shell-wise catalytic surface compositions that outperform a chemically-agnostic baseline—exhibiting lower Fréchet Distance, lower Leakage, and higher Cosine Similarity—and will require less training data and adapt effectively via LoRA during fine-tuning.
